title: Global Markets Brief — 01 Jun 2026
intro: Risk appetite remains constructive in early Asian trade as markets digest a dovish pivot from major central banks amid cooling inflation data. The USD shows mixed performance with JPY weakness persisting on widening rate differentials, while commodities benefit from supply concerns and renewed China demand optimism following stronger manufacturing PMI data.

sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk appetite remains constructive in early Asian trade as markets digest a dovish pivot from major central banks amid cooling inflation data. The USD shows mixed performance with JPY weakness persisting on widening rate differentials, while commodities benefit from supply concerns and renewed China demand optimism following stronger manufacturing PMI data.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
Strongest to Weakest: CHF (+0.27%) | CAD (+0.12%) | JPY (-0.13%) | USD | GBP (+0.04%) | AUD (+0.06%) | EUR (-0.04%) | NZD (-0.30%)
Safe-haven flows favor CHF amid European banking sector concerns. CAD benefits from energy rally. JPY remains under pressure despite intervention warnings.

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1654: Consolidating below 1.17 resistance. ECB dovish tilt weighs on single currency. Bias neutral with 1.16-1.17 range intact.
USD/JPY 159.47: Testing intervention zone as BoJ maintains ultra-loose policy. Ministry of Finance verbal warnings intensify. Risk of sharp reversal above 160.
GBP/USD 1.3462: Sterling finds support on BoE hawkish hold. Cable eyes 1.35 resistance amid improved UK data flow and political stability.
AUD/USD 0.7190: Commodity currency benefits from China PMI beat and iron ore strength. RBA pause supportive but USD resilience caps upside near 0.72.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Real money selling EUR on ECB dovish shift, targeting 1.15 medium-term
• Hedge funds accumulating JPY shorts despite intervention risk
• Corporate demand for CHF hedges accelerates on banking contagion fears
• EM currency outflows moderate as Fed pause expectations solidify

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Nikkei 225 leads (+0.79%) on weak yen boost to exporters. US markets advance with Dow (+0.72%) outperforming tech-heavy Nasdaq (+0.20%). European indices lag with FTSE 100 (-0.16%) pressured by banking sector rotation. Growth-value rotation continues favoring cyclicals over technology.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold 4552.60: Retreats from record highs on profit-taking but remains supported by central bank buying and geopolitical risk. Key support at 4500.
WTI Crude 89.69: Surges on Saudi production cut extension and China demand recovery. Targeting 92 resistance with inventory drawdowns supportive.
Silver 76.10: Industrial demand narrative supports modest gains. Ratio to gold remains elevated at historical extremes.
Natural Gas 3.369: Supply concerns drive rally ahead of summer cooling season. LNG export capacity constraints underpin prices.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC 73253: Consolidates below 75000 resistance after recent institutional inflows. Support at 70000 crucial for bull trend continuation.
ETH 1991.03: Underperforms BTC with ETH/BTC ratio at 0.027. Shanghai upgrade benefits fading as staking yields normalize.
Top Movers: XRP leads decline at 1.3157 (-1.17%) on regulatory overhang. SOL 81.60 (-0.85%) and DOGE 0.0999 (-0.34%) follow broader alt weakness.

sections.heading: 📈 LONG
sections.content:
• WTI Crude: Supply cuts drive momentum toward 92
• USD/CHF: Risk-on flows target 0.79 resistance
• Nikkei 225: Weak yen benefits exporters, eye 67500
📉 SHORT
• EUR/USD: ECB dovish pivot targets 1.16 support
• Gold: Overbought conditions warrant correction to 4500
• XRP: Regulatory uncertainty pressures altcoin leadership

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: US ISM Manufacturing PMI (14:00 GMT) expected at 48.5. China Caixin PMI already beat at 51.7. Monitor 10Y Treasury yields at 3.85% for breakout signals. ECB's Lagarde speaks at 16:30 GMT on monetary policy outlook.
